persons.arguments_against: Possible objections: (1) the surname spellings differ (GALIWEY vs Gallwey) — but this is a one-character OCR substitution of 'iw' for 'lw' in a single-edition entry, and the forename string 'Thomas Lionel J(ohn)' matches in full; (2) the deterministic honour check fails — but the stint record is from 1883 and the K.C.M.G. was conferred in 1889, the year after the stint ends, so the absence of a printed honour in 1883 is exactly what a true match predicts; (3) a same-name relative — but the Governorship and Commander-in-Chiefship of Bermuda is a unique office held by one person at a time, and the biography dates his tenure 1882-88, enclosing the stint's 1883-1888 listing precisely. No counter-hypothesis survives.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence that a different T. L. J. Gallwey governed Bermuda in 1883-88, or that the biography's 'gov. and comdr.-in-ch. of Bermuda, 1882-88' is itself an OCR conflation of two entries.

notes: Unique gubernatorial office, exact tenure containment (1882-88 bio vs 1883-1888 stint), full three-forename agreement. Bio surname 'GALIWEY' is an OCR error for 'Gallwey'.
